articles.title: Europe's aluminium roadmap: circularity, resource security, low-carbon imperative
articles.summary: AlCircle analysis maps how Europe's aluminium industry is anchoring its long-term strategy on circularity, resource security, and a low-carbon imperative, with closed-loop recycling systems increasingly becoming standard practice across the continent's production base.
articles.novelis_angle: With its European plants targeting 75% recycled content by 2030, Novelis is positioned ahead of the structural shift toward closed-loop mandates that Europe's roadmap foreshadows.

articles.title: Slovak PM Fico: we're finalising reopening of aluminium production in Žiar nad Hronom
articles.summary: Slovak PM Robert Fico announced that the restart of the Slovalco aluminium smelter in Žiar nad Hronom is in its final stages, with a formal announcement expected within weeks; the plant — a Hydro/Penta JV — has been idle since early 2023 after 70+ years of operation, and a government MoU signed in February 2026 targets a 2027 production restart.
articles.novelis_angle: A Slovalco restart would add primary P1020 supply back to the European market, with the potential to ease tightness that has kept the ECDP elevated near $565/t throughout 2026.

articles.title: Synchronised slight recovery in aluminium prices as China's destocking stabilises; upside room limited
articles.summary: LME and Chinese domestic aluminium prices recorded a slight synchronised recovery in mid-June as China's ingot destocking — which had drawn down inventory from a near-three-year peak — showed signs of stabilising; analysts see limited further upside from current levels.
